action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 16 completed training with final val_acc ~79.7%, far below the best eval of 83.11% (iteration 10). The training logs show train_acc plateaued at ~79.5% and loss ~1.63, indicating severe underfitting on a linear head. Analysis: The current train.py (truncated in workspace) almost certainly steps the learning-rate scheduler every batch instead of every epoch, causing the LR to collapse to near-zero after the first few batches and preventing convergence. The successful recipe from iteration 8 used per-epoch scheduling and reached ~82.4% (83.1% with TTA). Decision: Rewrite train.py from scratch with a clean, verified loop: scheduler.step() called once per epoch after train_epoch; keep all other proven hyperparameters (AdamW lr=1e-3, wd=1e-2, cosine annealing, label smoothing 0.1, feature noise 0.01, full feature replay, per-task head reinitialization, IN21k ViT backbone, 20 epochs). Run training, verify predict.py offline, and immediately submit eval to recover the 83% baseline.
